title: 10. CHARGE-OFFS AND RECOVERIES
content:
Charge-Offs & Recoveries
(Charge-off and recovery data presented are as of December 31, 2026)
Year-to-date gross charge-offs totaled approximately $911K, representing a significant increase from prior-year levels. Losses remain heavily concentrated in all other unsecured loans and lines, which accounted for the vast majority of total charge-off activity.

Year-to-date recoveries were minimal at less than $50K, with negligible contributions across most segments due to limited recovery opportunities remaining on aged balances.

Although charge-off volume has increased notably, continued focus on collections, early-stage delinquency management, and portfolio monitoring remains important to sustaining favorable credit performance.

Allowance & CECL Position
The CECL allowance totals approximately $2.985M ($2,985K), reflecting a 13.4% decline from the previous quarter-end. Reserve levels remain aligned with current portfolio risk characteristics and recent charge-off trends, though provisioning direction reflects ongoing adjustments to account for elevated loss rates in unsecured segments.

Reserve levels address:
- Risk concentrations within unsecured loan portfolios
- Recent delinquency acceleration across consumer lending lines
- Expected future credit conditions under prevailing economic assumptions

Management continues to evaluate CECL assumptions and loss factors to ensure reserve adequacy remains appropriate for the evolving risk profile of the loan portfolio.

Overall Assessment
Loan performance reflects heightened credit pressure despite continued contraction in overall exposure. Delinquencies remain elevated within unsecured lending segments, while charge-off activity has increased substantially compared with prior periods. The portfolio continues to be supported by strong capital and adjusted reserve coverage levels that reflect current loss dynamics. Management remains focused on disciplined underwriting standards, proactive collection efforts, and ongoing monitoring of emerging credit risks to preserve asset quality while supporting future loan growth and earnings stability.
